name: Apple iPad Pro 13-inch (M4)
introText: The iPad Pro 13-inch with M4 has the best display of any tablet ever made — Ultra Retina XDR OLED with tandem stack technology — in the thinnest tablet Apple has ever built at $1,599 CAD.
whatWorks: Tandem OLED display is the best screen on any tablet — two OLED panels stacked produce 1000 nits sustained brightness without OLED's traditional brightness limitations, perfect for outdoor and HDR use.
M4 chip is laptop-class and future-proofed for a decade — the iPad Pro M4 is faster than most Windows laptops sold today; it will receive iPadOS updates for 6+ years.
worthKnowing: iPadOS still limits desktop-class workflows — despite laptop-level hardware, iPadOS app and windowing limitations mean the iPad Pro can't fully replace a MacBook for many professional use cases.
Accessories add significant cost — Apple Pencil Pro ($149) and Magic Keyboard ($479) can add $628 CAD to the purchase, pushing the total past $2,200.

commonComplaints.title: iPadOS limits the hardware's potential
commonComplaints.body: The M4 chip is desktop-class but iPadOS apps and file management don't fully exploit it. For $1,600, many professionals choose a MacBook Air instead.

commonComplaints.title: Accessories mandatory for productivity
commonComplaints.body: Without the Apple Pencil ($149) and Magic Keyboard ($479), the iPad Pro is essentially an expensive media consumption device.

commonComplaints.title: Base 256GB feels thin for Pro
commonComplaints.body: Professional creative work quickly fills 256GB — the 512GB model ($1,999+) is recommended for video and photography.

verdict: The iPad Pro 13 M4 is the best tablet available — the display is extraordinary, the performance is desktop-class, and the Apple Pencil experience is unmatched. If you're a professional artist, architect, or musician who works in iPad-native apps, nothing else comes close. For general productivity, the iPad Air 13 saves $500.

realBuyers.quote: Professional illustrator. The ProMotion display and Apple Pencil Pro make this my primary work device. Nothing competes.
The OLED display is jaw-dropping. Watching HDR content on this is better than my TV.
Worth every dollar for Procreate work. The M4 chip handles 200+ layer files without blinking.

sources.title: iPad Pro M4 Review — The Verge

metaDescription: Apple iPad Pro 13 M4 review Canada: Ultra Retina XDR OLED, 120Hz, M4 chip. $1,599 CAD. Best tablet available for Canadian professionals and artists.
